title: Indulgent Irish Cheese Flatbread
description: Charred, cheese-pulled flatbread with shio-koji and chervil, served from a binchotan grill for a touch of Irish date-night charm.
instructions: Knead the flour, yeast, salt, sugar, warm water, and olive oil into a smooth dough and let it rise for 20 minutes.
Roll the dough into a flat circle about 12 inches in diameter.
Brush the surface lightly with olive oil, then sprinkle evenly with shio-koji and chopped chervil.
Place the flatbread on a preheated binchotan grill and cook for 6 minutes until the bottom char marks appear.
Flip the bread and cook the other side for 4 minutes, then top with the mixed cheese blend and caramelized onions.
Continue grilling for an additional 2 minutes until the cheese melts into a pullable, gooey layer.
Remove the flatbread, drizzle with balsamic glaze, slice, and serve immediately.
